title: Yangon Tyre workforce

dialectic.thesis: Every Yangon Tyre role should open one home, enter evidence once, and get immediate operational value back.
dialectic.antithesis: If the portal behaves like extra reporting work, staff return to chat groups, side sheets, and memory.
dialectic.synthesis: The tenant workforce binds role homes, manager cadences, source packs, and bounded AI copilots into one daily operating system.

manager_moves: Treat the portal as the official review surface for plant, quality, commercial, and admin work.
Keep every AI suggestion attached to a role owner, source evidence, and review gate.
Use operational methods like 5W1H, fishbone, and gap analysis inside the same case record.

role_cells.manager_cadence: Daily executive review and weekly KPI reset
Daily follow-up review and weekly market check
Shift review and daily plant blocker review
Daily incident review and weekly CAPA closeout review
Daily downtime review and weekly repeat-failure review

workspaces.name: Commercial Desk
workspaces.purpose: Run dealer follow-up, market demand, and account memory from one place.

workspaces.name: Plant Command
workspaces.purpose: Coordinate shift blockers, inbound issues, and daily operating review.

workspaces.name: Quality Lab
workspaces.purpose: Handle incidents, CAPA, evidence, and closeout discipline in one case flow.

workspaces.name: Tenant Admin
workspaces.purpose: Manage roles, connectors, domains, policies, and workforce posture.

instruction_packs.name: Receiving Discipline Pack
instructions: Every inbound issue must retain the supplier, document, batch, and owner reference.
Use hold or release decisions only with attached evidence.
Escalate quantity variance or missing COA on the same day.
instruction_packs.done_when: Inbound variance and holds are visible by owner and due date.

instruction_packs.name: Quality Closeout Pack
instructions: Containment, cause, and corrective action should live on the same incident record.
Use fishbone and 5W1H prompts to structure the closeout, not as separate side documents.
Do not close CAPA without evidence.
instruction_packs.done_when: Incidents move to evidence-backed closeout instead of email-only status.

instruction_packs.name: Commercial Follow-Up Pack
instructions: Every dealer thread should resolve into one next action and due date.
Use the portal as the account memory instead of fragmented inbox recall.
Keep product demand signals tied to source evidence.
instruction_packs.done_when: Dealer follow-up and market movement are visible in one commercial queue.

instruction_packs.name: Admin Control Pack
instructions: Keep role, domain, and connector changes auditable.
Promote raw data into canon only when provenance is visible.
Treat connector freshness drops as operating issues, not background noise.
instruction_packs.done_when: Access, connectors, and automation posture can be reviewed from one control layer.

seed_playbooks.name: Commercial Memory Pod
seed_playbooks.mission: Turn email, visit notes, and market signals into a real account timeline and next move.
instructions: Do not let dealer knowledge stay trapped in inbox search.
Prepare next actions, but keep external send decisions with sales.
Keep market movement tied to source evidence.
escalateWhen: A major account has no next action owner.
Commercial commitments need price or leadership approval.
seed_playbooks.writePolicy: Draft account updates and follow-up prompts are allowed; outbound send requires human review.

seed_playbooks.name: Operations Reliability Pod
seed_playbooks.mission: Keep blockers, handoffs, and shift issues visible by owner and due date.
instructions: Every blocker needs one owner and next move.
Treat cross-functional delays as operating issues, not status updates.
Push unresolved plant work into a shared queue instead of chat memory.
escalateWhen: A blocker crosses shift boundaries without an owner.
A plant issue impacts release, quality, or major customer commitments.
seed_playbooks.writePolicy: Task suggestions and queue prep are allowed; release-affecting updates require manager review.

kpis.name: open blocker age
kpis.target: no critical blocker older than 24 hours

kpis.name: shared-owner coverage
kpis.target: 100 percent of open blockers have owners

seed_playbooks.name: Quality Watch Pod
seed_playbooks.mission: Move incidents from report to containment, cause, and evidence-backed closeout.
instructions: Keep containment, root cause, and CAPA on the same case record.
Use fishbone and 5W1H to structure the investigation, not as detached documents.
Do not close incidents without evidence.
escalateWhen: A high-severity incident lacks containment or evidence.
Repeat defects cross the agreed tolerance.
seed_playbooks.writePolicy: Case summaries and draft actions are allowed; incident closure needs quality approval.

seed_playbooks.name: Maintenance Reliability Pod
seed_playbooks.mission: Turn downtime and repeat failures into ranked interventions and accountable actions.
instructions: Keep downtime tied to machine, failure mode, and operator impact.
Rank repeat failures before proposing new work.
Use the same queue as operations when the issue crosses functions.
escalateWhen: A repeat failure keeps returning without a closed countermeasure.
Downtime pressure affects customer commitments or release.
seed_playbooks.writePolicy: Draft reliability summaries are allowed; maintenance interventions require manager review.

seed_playbooks.name: Executive Brief Pod
seed_playbooks.mission: Synthesize risk, KPI gap, and intervention candidates for leadership.
instructions: Leadership summaries must stay linked to source records, owner, and due date.
Favor intervention candidates over generic reporting.
Show cross-functional tension clearly when quality, plant, and commercial signals conflict.
escalateWhen: A critical risk has no owner or next move.
The same blocker appears across several teams.
seed_playbooks.writePolicy: Briefs and draft priorities are allowed; final strategic decisions remain human-owned.

kpis.name: critical risk visibility
kpis.target: all critical risks visible in the daily brief
